title: The Need for PPE (Q&A)
description: Identify Personal Protective Equipment and understand what each item protects against.

questions.prompt: Catch the necessary Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) required to safely enter the workshop, but avoid catching any hazards!

items.explanation: Safety Goggles protect your eyes from flying debris, sparks, and chemical splashes.
Ear Defenders are essential when working around loud machinery to prevent long-term hearing loss.
Steel-toe Safety Boots protect your feet from heavy falling objects and sharp materials.
High-Visibility Clothing ensures you are easily seen by vehicle operators and colleagues.
Dust masks and respirators protect your lungs from inhaling harmful fumes, dust, and particulates.
Hard hats protect your head from falling objects and overhead hazards.
Protective Gloves guard your hands against cuts, abrasions, and chemical burns.
Standard shoes expose your feet to heavy falling objects, sharp swarf, and chemical spills! Never wear them in a workshop.
Loose clothing and ties can get caught in rotating machinery like lathes or drills, causing catastrophic injuries.
Listening to music prevents you from hearing fire alarms, warnings from colleagues, or the sound of failing machinery.
Eating in a workshop is dangerous because your food can become contaminated by dust, swarf, or toxic chemicals.
Toys and inflatables are a major distraction and tripping hazard in a busy workshop environment.
Standard glasses shatter easily upon impact and do not protect against fast-flying debris from the sides.
